rsmod_api_status: needs implementation - api/hunt handles NPC hunting but aggressiveness tolerance timer not confirmed

overview.description: Aggressive NPCs will automatically attack players within their aggro range without being provoked. This is a fundamental combat mechanic affecting NPC behavior.
overview.aggro_range: NPCs will auto-attack players within a certain number of tiles (usually 8 tiles for most monsters)
overview.tolerance_mechanic: After spending ~10 minutes (approx 1000 ticks) in the area of aggressive NPCs, they become tolerant and stop auto-attacking the player

tolerance_system.description: Players can 'reset' NPC tolerance by leaving the area and returning
tolerance_system.area_definition: Typically defined per-region or per-NPC group
tolerance_system.reset_method: Leave the area (varies - usually 1-2 map regions away) and return
exceptions: Some NPCs (like Skeletal Wyverns, Maniacal monkeys) never become tolerant
Wilderness NPCs may have different tolerance rules

aggression_range.description: Most aggressive NPCs will attack players within 8 tiles of them

special_cases.boss_npcs: Usually 5-6 tiles or triggered by entering their room
special_cases.stationary_guards: May have wider or narrower ranges per design

required: Player must be within aggro range
NPC must not be in combat with another player
Player must not have tolerance immunity active for this NPC group
NPC must have line of sight to player
optional_conditions: Some NPCs only aggro in multi-combat zones
Wilderness NPCs may only aggro above specific Wilderness levels
Some NPCs only aggro players below a combat level threshold

combat_level_threshold.description: Some NPCs only attack players whose combat level is lower than a threshold
combat_level_threshold.formula: NPC will only attack if player_combat_level < npc_combat_level * 2
exceptions: Cave and dungeon specific NPCs (often no threshold)

wander_radius.description: NPCs have a defined wander radius from their spawn point. They will not move beyond this radius.
notes: Aggressive NPCs will chase players to the edge of their wander radius
NPCs reset to spawn point if player leaves wander radius while in combat
Some NPCs have wander radius of 0 (stationary)
Maximum wander radius is typically 64 tiles

multi_combat_aggression.description: Multiple NPCs can engage the same player in multi-combat areas
multi_combat_aggression.single_combat: Only one NPC may attack a single player at a time

implementation_notes.rsmod_api_status: api/hunt contains NPC target finding logic, but tolerance system needs full implementation
rsmod_gaps: Tolerance timer per NPC group (10-minute immunity system) needs implementing
Combat level threshold check for aggression (player_cb < npc_cb * 2) needs verification
NPC wander radius enforcement during chase needs testing
Multi vs single combat zone awareness for aggression needs review
rsmod_implemented: Line of sight check for attack
rsmod_needed: 10-minute tolerance timer system keyed per area/region
Reset tolerance when player leaves defined area
Combat level threshold: only aggro if player_cb < npc_cb * 2

varbit_references.notes: No specific varbit for tolerance found - likely tracking server-side per player in area data
